mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes that allows you to back up cluster resources and persistent volumes, and migrate them across clusters. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and migration scenarios. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration of storage backends and potential complexities in restoring large volumes of data.

steps.title: Add Velero Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Velero to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/velero/releases/download/v1.18.0/velero-crds.yaml

```

> This command fetches and applies the CRDs for Velero.

steps.title: Install Velero
steps.description:
This step installs Velero using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_REGION>

```

> Replace <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> and <YOUR_REGION> with your actual AWS S3 bucket name and region.
commands: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_REGION>

steps.title: Create a Backup
steps.description:
This step creates a backup of your Kubernetes resources using Velero.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces <YOUR_NAMESPACE>

```

> Replace <YOUR_NAMESPACE> with the namespace you want to back up.

steps.title: Verify Velero Installation
steps.description:
This step checks if Velero is installed correctly and running.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

velero backup get

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.
> This command lists all backups to verify the backup creation.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all backups and associated resources, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command uninstalls the Velero release.
> This command deletes the Velero namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current state. This step includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading Velero, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

velero backup create pre-upgrade-backup --include-namespaces <YOUR_NAMESPACE>

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Create a backup before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that the Velero pod is running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to incorrect bucket permissions or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the S3 bucket exists and that the IAM role has the necessary permissions.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Velero server is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Velero server pod may have failed to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Velero pod using 'kubectl logs <velero-pod-name> -n velero' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Backup not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The backup may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Run 'velero backup get' to check the status of backups and ensure the backup command was executed correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'VolumeSnapshot not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the volume snapshot location is not configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the volume snapshot location configuration in the Velero installation.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.25+, improved AWS S3 integration, and enhanced error messages.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for Kubernetes versions below 1.25.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17 or earlier, ensure to update your S3 bucket policies to align with new permissions.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' and the output of 'velero backup get' should list your backups.
codeSnippets: helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0 --set configuration.provider=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.name=aws --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.bucket=<YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --set configuration.backupStorageLocation.prefix=velero --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.name=aws --set configuration.volumeSnapshotLocation.region=<YOUR_REGION>

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources and tools installed.
